perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS PNC produces, distributes, markets and sells products including ready to drink protein shakes and beverages, powdered protein shakes, nutrition bars, and dietary supplements (the "Finished Products"); and WHEREAS Supplier produces raw materials including protein powders used by PNC to produce at least some of the Finished Products; NOW THEREFORE in consideration of their respective rights and obligations as set forth in this Agreement, and for other good and valuable consideration, the adequacy and receipt of which are acknowledged, PNC and Supplier agree as follows: 1 Supply of Ingredients 1.1 Supplier will provide such materials to PNC or its Third Party Manufacturers ("TPMs") as are specified in any Master Purchase Commitment or any other purchase orders that the Parties may execute from time to time during the term of this Agreement ("Ingredients"). Ingredients will be produced at Supplier's facilities listed in a Master Purchase Commitment, or any other of Supplier's facilities approved in advance, in writing by PNC.
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS PNC produces, distributes, markets and sells products including ready to drink protein shakes and beverages, powdered protein shakes, nutrition bars, and dietary supplements (the "Finished Products"); and WHEREAS Supplier produces raw materials including protein powders used by PNC to produce at least some of the Finished Products; NOW THEREFORE in consideration of their respective rights and obligations as set forth in this Agreement, and for other good and valuable consideration, the adequacy and receipt of which are acknowledged, PNC and Supplier agree as follows: 1 Supply of Goods 1.1 Supplier will provide such materials to PNC or its Third Party Manufacturers ("TPMs") as are specified in any Master Purchase Commitment or any other purchase orders that the Parties may execute from time to time during the term of this Agreement ("Ingredients"). Ingredients will be produced at Supplier's facilities listed in a Master Purchase Commitment, or any other of Supplier's facilities approved in advance, in writing by PNC.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Supply of Ingredients" to "Supply of Goods" in Section 1 creates ambiguity. Later sections refer specifically to "Ingredients." This raises the question of whether the "goods" referred to in Section 1 encompass anything beyond the Ingredients defined later in the original section. For instance, could it include packaging materials or other items not explicitly defined as 'Ingredients'? This creates uncertainty as to the scope of the Supplier's obligations under Section 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Supplier will provide such materials to PNC or its Third Party Manufacturers ("TPMs") as are specified in any Master Purchase Commitment or any other purchase orders that the Parties may execute from time to time during the term of this Agreement ("Ingredients"). Ingredients will be produced at Supplier's facilities listed in a Master Purchase Commitment, or any other of Supplier's facilities approved in advance, in writing by PNC.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Supply of Ingredients" to "Supply of Goods" creates a contradiction. While the subsequent paragraph defines "Ingredients," the term "Goods" is broader and undefined. This conflicts with the contradicted text because the supplier's obligation is now unclear; it could be interpreted as supplying items beyond the specific "Ingredients" mentioned, creating ambiguity and a potential inconsistency regarding the supplier's precise obligations. The Misaligned Terminology definition clearly states this creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Supplier will notify Buyer immediately via person-to- person voice communication in the event that any pathogen is found, or reasonably suspected, in the plant environment during any environmental monitoring activity that could have an impact on the quality or safety of PNC's Ingredients. In the event of an actual or suspected food safety concern, Supplier shall conduct sampling in all relevant areas and promptly provide results of such tests to PNC. If PNC or its representatives find that any of Supplier's facilities, processes, inventory, procedures or equipment are not in accordance or compliance with the requirements of this Agreement or applicable law or regulation, PNC will give notice to Supplier, and Supplier shall promptly take all reasonable steps to correct such deficiency as soon as possible.
perturbation.changed_text: Supplier will notify Buyer immediately via person-to- person voice communication in the event that any pathogen is found, or reasonably suspected, in the plant environment during any environmental monitoring activity that could have an impact on the quality or safety of PNC's Ingredients. In the event of an actual or suspected food safety issue, Supplier shall conduct sampling in all relevant areas and promptly provide results of such tests to PNC. If PNC or its representatives find that any of Supplier's facilities, processes, inventory, procedures or equipment are not in accordance or compliance with the requirements of this Agreement or applicable law or regulation, PNC will give notice to Supplier, and Supplier shall promptly take all reasonable steps to correct such deficiency as soon as possible.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "food safety concern" to "food safety issue" introduces a subtle but potentially significant ambiguity. While seemingly synonymous, "concern" implies a broader range of potential problems, including those that may not yet be fully substantiated, while "issue" suggests a more concrete, confirmed problem. This creates confusion on what exactly defines an 'issue' versus a 'concern', making it ambiguous to determine what triggers the obligation to conduct sampling and provide test results to PNC.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Supplier will notify Buyer immediately via person-to- person voice communication in the event that any pathogen is found, or reasonably suspected, in the plant environment during any environmental monitoring activity that could have an impact on the quality or safety of PNC's Ingredients. In the event of an actual or suspected food safety issue, Supplier shall conduct sampling in all relevant areas and promptly provide results of such tests to PNC. If PNC or its representatives find that any of Supplier's facilities, processes, inventory, procedures or equipment are not in accordance or compliance with the requirements of this Agreement or applicable law or regulation, PNC will give notice to Supplier, and Supplier shall promptly take all reasonable steps to correct such deficiency as soon as possible.
perturbation.justification: While the change from "food safety concern" to "food safety issue" introduces a potential ambiguity, it doesn't create a direct, irreconcilable contradiction with the contradicted text. Both terms, even with their subtle differences, still trigger the sampling obligation. A reasonable reader could interpret them as substantially similar in this context, and it's not immediately clear that the change would render any part of the agreement unenforceable or create a significant legal issue. The contradicted text section is in the same location as the changed text, and although there's a potential for ambiguity, it's not a strong enough contradiction to warrant a 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: Supplier will comply with all applicable environmental laws and reporting obligations, maintain all required permits, and strive to responsibly manage the impacts of their operations on the environment.
perturbation.changed_text: Supplier will comply with all applicable environmental laws and reporting obligations, maintain all required permits, and strive to responsibly manage the impacts of their operations on planetary health.
perturbation.explanation: Switching 'environment' to 'planetary health' in Section 19 creates a vague expansion of scope, where the term 'planetary health' in the context of a Master Supply Agreement is not clear, while 'environment' is generally understood. The obligations related to 'planetary health' are not clearly defined, allowing for potential disputes about the extent of Supplier's responsibilities. The rest of the contract refers only to the 'environment', so this change creates an internal inconsistency.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Supplier will comply with all applicable environmental laws and reporting obligations, maintain all required permits, and strive to responsibly manage the impacts of their operations on planetary health.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'environment' to 'planetary health' introduces a vague and undefined term that contradicts the established understanding of 'environment' used elsewhere in the contract. The 'Contradicted Text' mentions that 'Supplier will comply with all applicable environmental laws and reporting obligations, maintain all required permits, and strive to responsibly manage the impacts of their operations on planetary health'. Although the Contradicted Text has the same language as the Changed Text, the Original Text differs. This implies that there is a change to the overall document, and the term 'planetary health' has not been previously defined and the extent of the obligations is unclear, which creates ambiguity where the original contract expected a clear, commonly understood definition of 'environment'. This falls under the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology' where different terms ('environment' and 'planetary health') are used interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.
